blocks.content: Grafana Alloy uses a declarative configuration file to describe what Alloy should run. This learning path introduces the small set of syntax concepts you need before you build a pipeline.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this path, you'll be able to:

1. Identify attributes, expressions, and blocks in an Alloy configuration
1. Explain what a component is
1. Distinguish component arguments from exports
1. Trace an export from one component into another component's argument
1. Explain why component references can't form a cycle

## Before you begin

To run the short configuration example, you need:

- [Grafana Alloy installed](https://grafana.com/docs/alloy/latest/set-up/install/)
- Permission to create and edit a file named `config.alloy`
- A terminal where you can run Alloy

You don't need Grafana Cloud, Prometheus, Loki, or an existing telemetry pipeline.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back! Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

We understand you might want to explore other capabilities not strictly on this path. We'll provide you opportunities where it makes sense.
